design.summary: M17 = three sleep-time memory daemons (CONSOLIDATE / REFLECT / FORGET) realized as ONE bounded maintenance cycle driven through the already-wired M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20 method (Rights::CONSOLIDATE=1<<9), extending crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs in pure safe Rust over the existing M13 structures — ZERO new unsafe (mem.rs keeps its line-1 #![forbid(unsafe_code)]). No new ABI method numbers (the M11 closed set stays frozen; the M16 'method 33 == BadMethod' probe is untouched); M17 only widens the op-selector space of consolidate(op,a,b,c). The cycle wakes on the GA importance-accumulator (>=150) or scheduler-idle, does fixed bounded work then yields. CONSOLIDATE dedup/merges near-duplicate T3 records into a durable distilled survivor with supersedes+cites links (tombstoning only the DERIVED T3 duplicate, never the T2 source). REFLECT folds the recent high-salience slice into an insight T3 record with cites-back links — deterministic digest in mem.rs by default, with the M16 model: bridge wired NOW (not deferred) at the daemon-task layer via the existing mock (digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE). FORGET evaluates the existing fixed-point ACT-R BLA(d=0.5) bla_raw(count,age) and DEMOTES (tier 3->5, dropped from recall STAGE 1 but still M_MEM_READ-addressable over T2) only records that are SIMULTANEOUSLY stale AND low-importance AND low-utility AND past a grace window — the T2 append-only journal is NEVER popped, truncated, or age-tombstoned. Self-test slots between the 'M16: infer OK' marker (main.rs ~L1998) and halt(), drives op=3 synchronously (WITNESS-A idiom), asserts distill+reflect+demote+T2-intact+CONSOLIDATE-gated-denial, and prints 'M17: consolidate OK'.
design.daemon_cycle:
REALIZATION: an M9-scheduled BULK kernel task via the existing scheduler_spawn(STACK_DAEMON.take(), daemon_entry) (lib.rs ~L829). daemon_entry calls irq_unmask() once (the spin_c/spin_d preemptible pattern, main.rs ~L2144) then loops: on each wake it reads the trigger, and on a fire drives ONE bounded cycle via agent_consolidate_cycle(daemon_task) then yields. It is the lowest-priority runnable (round-robin today; the INTERACTIVE/PIPELINE/BULK QoS lane is the documented deferred hook inside schedule()), so any foreground INTERACTIVE task preempts it on the next tick — that is what keeps foreground p95 recall under budget DURING consolidation.

TRIGGER MODEL (no wall-clock cron; MEMORY-SPEC s4): add to MemSubstrate the fields imp_accum:u32 + last_consolidated_epoch:u64 + consol_cursor:u64, and the const IMP_ACCUM_THRESHOLD:u32 = 150 (GA). add() (mem.rs ~L385) gains one safe line after computing imp: `self.imp_accum = self.imp_accum.saturating_add(imp as u32);`. PRIMARY trigger = over_threshold() == (imp_accum >= 150). SECONDARY = scheduler-idle (no foreground runnable). TERTIARY = memory pressure (quota.records near a watermark, or quota.tokens_written approaching TOKEN_QUOTA). The daemon checks PRIMARY/SECONDARY on each wake.

BOUNDED WORK PER CYCLE: consolidation_cycle() does distill() (cap DISTILL_BATCH=32 clusters) + reflect(0) (cap REFLECT_FANOUT insights, bounded by TOKEN_QUOTA) + forget_sweep() (cap SWEEP_BATCH=64 records from a persisted consol_cursor that wraps), then resets imp_accum=0, sets last_consolidated_epoch=clock, calls backing.flush() (advances the freshness epoch the foreground reads via the existing epoch() before trusting T3), and returns the aggregate affected count. Persisting consol_cursor (kswapd clock-hand) lets the next cycle resume — never the whole journal in one wake.

CI DETERMINISM: every M13-M16 self-test runs with the timer DISARMED (RefCell discipline). So the M17 marker drives the cycle SYNCHRONOUSLY kernel-side (op=3 over a WITNESS-A home) for the deterministic verdict. The real 'runs without blocking foreground' property is an OPTIONAL armed-window witness: scheduler_spawn the daemon, timer_rearm() briefly, confirm a foreground M_MEM_RECALL still returns while involuntary_switch_count() advances, then timer_disarm() before the verdict. Single-&mut-at-a-time: the daemon must not borrow_mut a substrate that overlaps a foreground dispatch on the SAME home, so the concurrency witness uses a separate home/agent.
design.consolidate:
GOAL: collapse near-duplicate live T3 MemRecords sharing a token into one durable distilled survivor with supersedes+cites links, advancing the freshness epoch, NEVER touching T2. (Today add() writes 1:1 T2->T3, so 'distill T2->T3' is concretely dedup/merge-within-T3 + link; re-deriving T3 from scratch would risk desyncing the lexical index and is rejected.)

NEW SAFE FIELDS on MemRecord (mem.rs ~L258): links:Vec<(u8,u64)> (kind,target_id; LINK_CITES=0, LINK_RELATES=1, LINK_SUPERSEDES=2), tier:u8 (TIER_HOT=3 default, TIER_COLD=5), provenance:u8 (0 episodic, 1 reflection, 2 distilled survivor). add() initializes links=Vec::new(), tier=3, provenance=0. Factor a private push_record(token,value,imp,provenance,links)->id helper shared by add()/reflect() so all three new fields are set in one place.

fn distill(&mut self) -> u64, TWO-PHASE to satisfy the borrow checker with zero unsafe:
 PHASE A (immutable): scan self.t3.lexical (BTreeMap = deterministic order); for each token collect live (t_invalid==0 && tier==3) posting indices; skip groups with <2; pick the SURVIVOR = max importance, tie-break by largest t_created, then smallest id; record (survivor_idx, Vec<loser_idx>). Cap at DISTILL_BATCH clusters.
 PHASE B (mutable): for each plan entry, for each loser: set records[loser].t_invalid = self.clock (T3-only tombstone of the DERIVED duplicate — do NOT mirror to T2, the loser's source episode stays valid/replayable); push (LINK_SUPERSEDES, loser.id) and (LINK_CITES, loser.id) onto records[survivor].links; set records[survivor].provenance = 2; merged += losers.len(). self.clock = clock.wrapping_add(1).
EFFECT: subsequent M_MEM_RECALL over the token returns only the survivor (losers carry t_invalid and are filtered in STAGE 1); the survivor carries auditable supersedes/cites pointers to every merged-away id. SCHEMA-FIT FAST PATH: because the survivor is updated in place (links appended) rather than a new node, T3 stays compact. INVARIANT: distill never pushes/pops t2.log and never sets Episode.t_invalid.

FILL op=1 (the link stub at mem.rs ~L549): consolidate op=1 with a=from_id, b=to_id, c=kind -> find records[*].id==a, push (c as u8, b) onto its links, return 1. This is the real supersedes/cites/relates mechanism the schema promised and the daemon-task model-bridge uses to attach cites to a model-written insight.
design.reflect:
DECISION: REFLECT calls the M16 model NOW (not deferred) — but the model pass lives at the DAEMON-TASK layer where the INVOKE_MODEL cap is, never inside mem.rs (which holds no model cap and must stay free of any infer.rs dependency to preserve the M11 MemoryHome/ModelSession separation). mem.rs provides a PURE deterministic digest + the cites-linked write; the model is an optional transform on that digest.

fn reflect(&mut self, model_token: u64) -> u64 (mem.rs):
 1. Build the recent high-salience slice: iterate self.t3.records in reverse, filter live (t_invalid==0 && tier==3), take REFLECT_WINDOW (e.g. 16); collect their ids into cites:Vec<u64>.
 2. Compute a deterministic digest with fixed-point integer folding (no float, replayable): `let mut digest = REFLECT_SEED; for r in slice { digest = digest.rotate_left(7) ^ r.token ^ (r.importance as u64); }`.
 3. insight_token = if model_token != 0 { model_token } else { digest }.
 4. Write the insight as a NEW record via push_record(insight_token, /*value*/insight_token, /*imp*/ a mid value, provenance=1, links = cites.map(|id| (LINK_CITES, id))). This appends a T2 episode AND a T3 record (so the insight is itself lossless + recallable), respecting TOKEN_QUOTA (if exhausted, return 0 — bounded, fail-soft). Return the new insight id.
 5. REPLAY-STRENGTHENS: bump each cited source's importance by +1 (saturating at 10) so reflected-upon memories resist FORGET. BOUNDED DEPTH: only cite sources with provenance != 1 (one reflection level for M17) to prevent runaway reflection trees.

DETERMINISTIC PATH (op=3 cycle): calls reflect(0) -> insight_token == digest, fully model-free, always-available, CI-green on any backend.
MODEL-BRIDGED PATH (wired now, demonstrated because the mock is deterministic): the daemon task, holding BOTH a MemoryHome cap and a model:mock/echo ModelSession (agent_model_open, lib.rs ~L1747, grants INVOKE_MODEL|READ), does: (a) op=6 reflect_digest (read-only, returns digest), (b) agent_model_dispatch(M_MODEL_INVOKE, sess, digest) -> mock returns digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE (infer.rs ~L170), (c) op=4 reflect with args[1]=that token -> writes the insight whose value == digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE. Real backends (model:anthropic/..., model:local/llama3) drop in behind the same InferBackend trait, and because REFLECT is off the latency path it MAY run a stronger model than the foreground (Letta asymmetry) — deferred, same seam.
design.forget:
PASSIVE-DECAY PRINCIPLE: do NOT add a mutable current_activation decremented each tick. Reuse the existing pure fixed-point bla_raw(count, age) (mem.rs ~L64) = ln_fixed(2*(count+1)) - ln_fixed(age+1)/2 in SCALE=1000 units (verbatim ACT-R/Petrov BLA d=0.5). The sweep merely EVALUATES the already-decaying score and acts — giving both senses of fixed point: integer arithmetic (no FPU, replayable) AND a mathematical fixed point (running it twice at one clock classifies identically; an already-demoted tier==5 record is skipped, so the 2nd pass is a no-op). Monotone KEEP->DEMOTE only; T2 unreachable.

fn forget_sweep(&mut self) -> u64 over a BOUNDED window [consol_cursor .. consol_cursor+SWEEP_BATCH):
 for each idx in window: let r = &records[idx]; if r.t_invalid != 0 || r.tier != 3 { continue }; let age = clock.saturating_sub(r.t_created); if age < MIN_AGE { continue } (grace: brand-new records immune); let bla = bla_raw(r.count, age); let util = (r.c_succ as i64 + 1) * SCALE / (r.c_use as i64 + 2) (EvolveR s, fixed-point; default counters give 500). DEMOTE iff ALL hold (AND-gate = high-value survival): bla < THETA_DEMOTE AND (r.importance as i64) < IMP_PIN AND util < UTIL_PIN. On demote: records[idx].tier = TIER_COLD (5); n += 1. Advance consol_cursor (wrap to 0 at end of records).

DEMOTE vs TOMBSTONE (orthogonal axes):
 - DEMOTE (tier 3->5) = capacity statement 'still true, just cold'. Add ONE line to recall STAGE 1 (mem.rs ~L464, right after the t_invalid filter): `if r.tier != 3 { continue; }`. The record leaves the hot recall candidate set BUT stays fully addressable — read(id) (mem.rs ~L450) scans t2.log by id with NO tier/t_invalid filter, so M_MEM_READ still returns its value (the swap-out, not free()). 
 - TOMBSTONE = validity statement 'no longer true / merged away' — set ONLY by distill (supersession) and op=0 (contradiction), NEVER by forget_sweep on age. This is the precise demote-vs-tombstone answer.

RECOMMENDED CONSTANTS (named consts beside SCALE/TOKEN_QUOTA, [OPEN] for the M17 bench harness): THETA_DEMOTE=-1000 (a count=0 record demotes once age>~30 ticks; because the BLA zero-crossing scales as 4*(count+1)^2, frequently-accessed records get a proportionally longer reprieve for free), IMP_PIN=8 (importance>=8 flashbulb-pinned, never demoted), UTIL_PIN=600 (s>=0.6 proven-useful, retained; default 500 stays demotable), MIN_AGE=16, SWEEP_BATCH=64. Absolute thresholds on raw bla_raw (NOT min-max over the population) so the sweep needs only one record at a time.

HARD FLOOR INVARIANT: forget_sweep NEVER pops/truncates t2.log, NEVER sets Episode.t_invalid, NEVER age-tombstones. The lossless T2 episodic floor is the replay escape hatch; over-aggressive distillation is always recoverable from raw episodes. True hard-delete stays a separate privileged, human-gated, compliance-only op OUTSIDE the daemon's authority. FORGET is model-free by design and must NOT hold INVOKE_MODEL.
design.abi:
PURELY ADDITIVE, NO new method numbers — the M11 closed method set stays frozen (M_BLOCK_READ=31 highest; the M16 'method 33 == BadMethod' / closed-set proof is untouched). All M17 work rides the EXISTING M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20, rights-gated by Rights::CONSOLIDATE=1<<9 (caps.rs required_right ~L412 and the dispatch branch ~L900 already route args[0..3] into consolidate()). caps.rs needs ZERO change.

Widen consolidate(&mut self, op, a, b, c) (un-underscore the currently-unused _b/_c) op-selector space (args[0]=op, args[1]=a, args[2]=b, args[3]=c):
 op=0 tombstone id a (EXISTING; T3+T2 t_invalid annotate)
 op=1 LINK from=a to=b kind=c (FILL the stub: push (c,b) onto record a.links; return 1)
 op=2 T1 reachability GC (EXISTING)
 op=3 consolidation_cycle = distill()+reflect(0)+forget_sweep(); returns aggregate affected count
 op=4 reflect(model_token = a); writes the insight record; returns the new insight id
 op=5 forget_sweep(); returns demoted count
 op=6 reflect_digest (READ-ONLY, no write); returns the deterministic digest over the recent slice (for the daemon-task model bridge)
 op=7 read imp_accum (READ-ONLY); returns the accumulator (observability for the >=150 assertion)
 op=8 link_count of record id a (READ-ONLY); returns records[a].links.len() (lets the self-test prove cites/supersedes without a new method)

lib.rs FACADES (kernel-side only, no new dispatch method): agent_consolidate_cycle(task) = agent_mem_dispatch(task, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, 3,0,0,0) (clones agent_mem_dispatch ~L1399, drives ap.memory_home through the chokepoint); optional agent_mem_accumulator(task) = op=7. The born-with home rights are READ|WRITE|RECALL (lib.rs ~L1288) WITHOUT CONSOLIDATE, so a normal agent's (e.g. agent_c's) cycle is correctly Denied; the daemon, when realized as a real task, either mints its own MemoryHome with CONSOLIDATE or the spawn path grows a wants_consolidate manifest flag. The model-bridged reflect rides the existing agent_model_open (~L1747) / agent_model_dispatch (~L1772). infer.rs needs ZERO change.
design.module_location:
PRIMARY: extend crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (keeps its line-1 #![forbid(unsafe_code)]) — add fields links/tier/provenance to MemRecord (~L258) and imp_accum/last_consolidated_epoch/consol_cursor to MemSubstrate (~L327); add consts IMP_ACCUM_THRESHOLD=150, THETA_DEMOTE=-1000, IMP_PIN=8, UTIL_PIN=600, MIN_AGE=16, SWEEP_BATCH=64, DISTILL_BATCH=32, REFLECT_WINDOW=16, REFLECT_FANOUT, LINK_CITES/RELATES/SUPERSEDES, TIER_HOT=3/TIER_COLD=5, REFLECT_SEED; add private fns push_record(), distill(), reflect(), forget_sweep(), consolidation_cycle(), over_threshold(); widen consolidate() op-space (ops 1/3/4/5/6/7/8); one-line edits to add() (imp_accum bump + new-field init via push_record) and recall() STAGE 1 (the `if r.tier != 3 { continue; }` demote skip). 
SECONDARY: crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs — add agent_consolidate_cycle(task) + optional agent_mem_accumulator(task) facades; add a STACK_DAEMON static + daemon_entry fn IF the armed-window concurrency witness is included (reuses scheduler_spawn/irq_unmask/timer_rearm/timer_disarm). 
SELF-TEST: kernel/src/main.rs — a new block between the 'M16: infer OK' marker (~L1998) and tb_hal::halt() (~L2002), mirroring the M13 WITNESS-A/B idiom with an m17_fail(why) helper and the disp() closure, ending with serial_write_str('M17: consolidate OK\n'); add a STACK_DAEMON static (~L118 neighborhood) if the concurrency witness is wired. 
UNCHANGED: crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs (method 20, right 1<<9, gate, dispatch branch all exist) and crates/tb-hal/src/infer.rs (reflect rides the existing model:mock/echo backend).
design.selftest:
Block in kernel/src/main.rs after 'M16: infer OK' (~L1998), before halt(); m17_fail(why) + the disp() closure + a WITNESS-A home minted with Rights::READ|WRITE|RECALL|CONSOLIDATE (its own fresh MemSubstrate). Concrete steps + asserts:
1. SEED DUP PAIR: disp(home, M_MEM_WRITE, [0, TOK_DUP, 0xD1, 9]) and [0, TOK_DUP, 0xD2, 9]) -> two live near-duplicate T3 records on the same token; capture id_a, id_b. (assert both Ok.)
2. SEED STALE: BEFORE the pair, disp(home, M_MEM_WRITE, [0, TOK_STALE, 0xCAFE, 1]) -> id_stale at records index 0, importance 1 (low). (Index 0 guarantees it is inside the first SWEEP_BATCH window.)
3. DRIVE ACCUMULATOR + AGE: a bounded loop `for _ in 0..40 { disp(home, M_MEM_WRITE, [0, TOK_NOISE, i, 9]) }` (importance 9 -> accumulator crosses 150; 40 writes advance clock so id_stale age > MIN_AGE and bla_raw(0, ~40) ~= -1163 < THETA_DEMOTE=-1000). OPTIONAL: assert agent op=7 (read imp_accum) >= 150 before the cycle.
4. CAPTURE next-id sentinel, then DRIVE CYCLE: disp(home, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, [3,0,0,0]) -> assert status==Ok and value>=1 (non-zero affected count).
5. ASSERT DISTILL: disp(home, M_MEM_RECALL, [TOK_DUP, 0, 1, 0]) -> Ok, resolves to a MemoryRecord (M_OBJECT_INSPECT==ObjKind::MemoryRecord), M_MEM_READ of that recalled id returns the SURVIVOR id (the loser is t_invalid -> filtered); disp(home, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, [8, survivor_id, 0, 0]) (op=8 link_count) >= 1 (supersedes/cites attached).
6. ASSERT REFLECT (deterministic): disp(home, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, [4, 0, 0, 0]) -> Ok, insight_id (>= the captured sentinel); disp(home, M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, [8, insight_id, 0, 0]) >= 1 (cites links present); M_MEM_READ[insight_id] returns the digest (non-zero). MODEL-BRIDGED: op=6 -> digest; agent_model_open(agent_c,'model:mock/echo'); agent_model_dispatch(M_MODEL_INVOKE, sess, digest) -> tok; op=4 with args[1]=tok -> insight2; assert M_MEM_READ[insight2] == digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE (deterministic mock).
7. ASSERT FORGET: id_stale is GONE from recall — disp(home, M_MEM_RECALL, [TOK_STALE, 0, 1, 0]).status != Ok (no live hot candidate) — YET still addressable: disp(home, M_MEM_READ, [id_stale, 0, 0, 0]) == 0xCAFE (demoted tier=5, T2 floor intact).
8. T2 FLOOR INTEGRITY: M_MEM_READ of an original dup/episode id post-cycle still returns its written value (nothing destroyed).
9. CONSOLIDATE-RIGHT-GATED DENIAL (two ways): disp(home_ro=mint(READ|RECALL), M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE, [3,0,0,0]).status == Denied; AND tb_hal::agent_consolidate_cycle(agent_c) -> Denied (agent_c born-with mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL lacks CONSOLIDATE, mirroring the existing M13 'agent C consolidate Denied' line).
10. OPTIONAL CONCURRENCY WITNESS: scheduler_spawn(STACK_DAEMON.take(), daemon_entry); timer_rearm(); confirm a foreground M_MEM_RECALL returns within the window while involuntary_switch_count() advances; timer_disarm() BEFORE the verdict.
Print intermediate lines ('mem: distilled + reflected + demoted, T2 floor intact') like M13, re-print all M2-M16 markers (cumulative DoD), end with 'M17: consolidate OK\n'.
design.new_unsafe: ZERO new unsafe — CONFIRMED. crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs keeps its line-1 #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and that attribute makes any accidental unsafe a COMPILE error, so the invariant is machine-enforced, not aspirational. Every M17 operation is a safe mutation of the existing safe structures: pushing onto Vec<MemRecord>/Vec<Episode>/Vec<(u8,u64)> links, inserting/iterating the BTreeMap lexical index, setting u8/bool/u64 fields (tier, provenance, t_invalid, imp_accum, consol_cursor), and integer arithmetic through the existing log2_fixed/ln_fixed/bla_raw path (no float, no FPU, no raw pointers, no transmute). The two-phase distill() reads an immutable plan then applies mutations in a separate scope, so the borrow checker is satisfied with safe code (no unsafe aliasing escape). caps.rs and infer.rs are unchanged. lib.rs facades only call existing safe APIs (agent_mem_dispatch, scheduler_spawn, agent_model_*). kernel/src/main.rs already carries localized arch unsafe for register lifts unrelated to M17; the M17 self-test block adds only safe disp()/facade calls. Net new unsafe lines across the whole milestone: 0.
risks: T2 lossless-floor destruction: distill() and forget_sweep() must NEVER push/pop/truncate t2.log nor set Episode.t_invalid. distill tombstones ONLY the derived T3 duplicate (records[loser].t_invalid), and forget DEMOTES via tier only. A demote that dropped the canonical record or a distill that touched T2 would break both the 'still addressable' guarantee and the lossless invariant. Mitigate with the op=8/read-back asserts in self-test steps 7-8.

Determinism / CI flakiness: the 150 accumulator and the BLA/importance demote thresholds are fixed-point integer consts; any clock or age off-by-one changes which records demote. The self-test must control aging deterministically (the bounded 0..40 noise-write loop with the stale record pinned at index 0 so it lands in the first SWEEP_BATCH window) and assert against absolute thresholds, not min-max-over-population.

Scheduled-daemon vs disarmed-timer discipline: M13-M16 run with the timer disarmed (RefCell reentrancy hazard). A real armed daemon must not borrow_mut a substrate overlapping a foreground dispatch on the SAME home. Mitigate: drive the marker synchronously over a WITNESS-A home; confine the armed-window concurrency witness to a separate agent/home; bound the cycle single-&mut-at-a-time.

Reflect model-seam scope creep: wiring the model INTO mem.rs would couple the substrate to infer.rs and force the daemon's INVOKE_MODEL cap through the wrong layer (breaking M11 MemoryHome/ModelSession separation). Keep mem.rs reflect a pure deterministic digest + write; do the model XOR transform at the daemon-task level via op=6 -> M_MODEL_INVOKE -> op=4.

ABI surface creep: adding a NEW method number instead of reusing M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op-selectors would perturb the M16 'method 33 == BadMethod' / frozen closed-set proof. All M17 ops (1/3/4/5/6/7/8) must stay under method 20.

Write-amplification quota interaction: reflect()/insight writes consume TOKEN_QUOTA; an unbounded reflection could exhaust it and fail-closed mid-cycle. Bound REFLECT_FANOUT and honor the existing quota check (return 0 / defer remainder to the next cycle) rather than panicking.

forget_sweep cursor coverage: with many records (e.g. the noise-write loop), SWEEP_BATCH=64 won't scan everything in one cycle; a target record beyond the window won't demote until the cursor wraps to it. Mitigate in-test by pinning the stale record at index 0; in production rely on the wrapping clock-hand cursor across cycles.

Distill survivor selection ambiguity: max-importance ties must be broken deterministically (largest t_created, then smallest id) or the merged-away set becomes nondeterministic and the link/recall asserts flake. The tie-break order must be fixed and documented.

util default pinning: util = (c_succ+1)*1000/(c_use+2) defaults to 500; UTIL_PIN must stay above it (600) or every fresh record is util-pinned and FORGET never demotes anything — the self-test demote step would never fire.

recon.m13_consolidation_hooks:
WHAT ALREADY EXISTS in crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (M13, all safe):
- MemSubstrate::consolidate(op,a,b,c) (~L520-552), CONSOLIDATE-gated via M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20. op=0 = tombstone id a: sets t_invalid=clock on BOTH the T3 MemRecord AND the T2 Episode (logical invalidation; the Vec entry is NEVER popped), flushes backing, returns affected count. op=1 = "add typed link" -- a NO-OP STUB returning 0 (link mechanism NOT implemented). op=2 = T1 reachability GC (t1.gc() truncates working-graph nodes to NODE_QUOTA; lossless because T2 holds the record).
- delete() (~L423): DELETE->tombstone primitive (t_invalid flip on most-recent live record for key).
- bla_raw(count,age) (~L64): the ACT-R/Ebbinghaus BLA(d=0.5) fixed-point DECAY itself -- freq=ln(2*(count+1)); recency=0.5*ln(age+1); BLA=freq-recency. But it is computed TRANSIENTLY only during recall ranking (~L486); never swept or stored as decayed state.
- MemRecord (~L256) carries the access-BLA state (count, last_ts[10], last_idx, written on the read path in recall ~L500), importance:u8 (1..10 GA poignancy), c_succ/c_use utility counters (DECLARED but never updated), t_created/t_invalid (bitemporal).
- recall STAGE 1 bitemporal asof filter (skips t_invalid!=0, ~L465) + Finsts exclude_recent.
- T2 append-only journal t2.log:Vec<Episode> (t_created/t_invalid/producing_task) -- the lossless floor, NEVER truncated by any op.
- Quota{tokens_written,records}, TOKEN_QUOTA write-amp cap.

WHAT M17 ADDS (the daemon driver + reflect + decay/demote sweep), all safe Rust over these structures:
1. Importance ACCUMULATOR + ~150 threshold trigger (MEMORY-SPEC s4 / GA 150) -- add() clamps importance but does NOT accumulate it anywhere; add an imp_accum counter + over_threshold() + last_consolidated_epoch.
2. DISTILL step -- today add() writes a 1:1 T3 record per T2 episode, so T3 is NOT distilled. M17 adds dedup/merge that collapses near-duplicate T3 records (same token / equivalent) into a durable semantic record.
3. LINK mechanism -- MemRecord has NO links field (the schema lists cites/relates/supersedes but the struct lacks it; op=1 is a stub). Add a typed links field/side-table to realize supersedes/cites; fill op=1.
4. REFLECT step -- synthesize a higher-level insight T3 record from recent episodes with cites links (absent).
5. FORGET sweep -- recompute bla_raw over live records and demote/tombstone low-activation+low-importance ones (the decay math exists; the threshold sweep + demote primitive do not).
6. DEMOTE primitive -- a demoted:bool / tier:u8 field so demoted records are excluded from recall STAGE 1 yet stay T2-addressable (only tombstone exists today).
7. The bounded consolidation_cycle()/reflect()/forget_sweep()/distill() driver methods.
recon.daemon_scheduling:
Realized as an M9 scheduled KERNEL TASK reusing the existing scheduler unchanged:
- scheduler_spawn(stack, entry) (lib.rs ~L829) reuses M2 task_create, appends the daemon's slot to RUNQUEUE; schedule() (~L847, wired via set_irq_hook) round-robins it on each timer tick. The daemon entry calls irq_unmask() once at the top to become preemptible -- exactly the M9 spin_c/spin_d pattern (main.rs ~L865, STACK_C/STACK_D.take()).
- BULK lane: the daemon is the lowest-priority runnable; round-robin today, the INTERACTIVE/PIPELINE/BULK QoS lane is the documented deferred hook inside schedule().
- TRIGGER: on each wake the daemon checks (a) the importance-accumulator overflow (~150) and/or (b) idle (no foreground runnable). On a fire it runs ONE bounded consolidation_cycle() (a fixed per-cycle scan budget: distill+reflect+forget) then yields -- bounded work per cycle, never the whole journal, so it never blocks the foreground critical path (the kswapd/dirty-page-writeback analogy).
- The daemon holds its OWN MemoryHome cap WITH the CONSOLIDATE right and drives the substrate through the M11 dispatch chokepoint (M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE), reaching it single-&mut-at-a-time exactly like agent_mem_dispatch -- no reentrant borrow_mut against a foreground dispatch on the same home.
- CI PRAGMATICS: every M13-M16 self-test runs with the timer DISARMED (synchronous, interrupts masked -- the RefCell discipline). So the deterministic M17 marker drives consolidation_cycle() SYNCHRONOUSLY kernel-side (the M13 WITNESS-A pattern), and OPTIONALLY proves the real "runs without blocking foreground" property by scheduler_spawn-ing the daemon in a briefly ARMED timer window (the M9 armed-window pattern) before timer_disarm() renders the verdict.
recon.reflect_model_seam:
Recommendation: reflect at M17 is importance-triggered (GA) and produces a cites-linked insight record; the model call is the SEAM, wired to the M16 deterministic mock, NOT deferred -- but kept OUT of mem.rs.
- DEFAULT in-substrate path: reflect() folds recent episode tokens into a digest/insight token by a PURE deterministic transform and writes it back as a new T3 record with cites links to its sources. This keeps the substrate model-agnostic, deterministic, zero new caps, zero new ABI.
- MODEL-BRIDGED path (demonstrated because the mock is deterministic): the DAEMON TASK can hold BOTH a MemoryHome cap and a model:mock/echo ModelSession cap (from the existing agent_model_open, lib.rs ~L1747, which grants INVOKE_MODEL|READ). It computes the digest, passes it through the mock via agent_model_dispatch(M_MODEL_INVOKE) (INVOKE_MODEL-gated; mock transform = prompt ^ 0xA110_C0DE, infer.rs MockBackend::infer), then writes the returned token back as the insight T3 record via M_MEM_WRITE. CI-deterministic, so the self-test can assert insight_token == digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE.
- Layering rule: do the model pass at the DAEMON-TASK level where the INVOKE_MODEL cap lives -- never inside mem.rs (which holds no model cap and must stay free of an infer.rs dependency, preserving the M11 cap separation between MemoryHome and ModelSession). Real backends (model:anthropic/..., model:local/llama3) drop in behind the same InferBackend trait, deferred.
recon.forget_policy:
BLA decay + threshold demote over the M13 records, two-level and floor-preserving:
- DECAY: reuse bla_raw(count, age) with age = clock - t_created (the existing Ebbinghaus/ACT-R fixed-point decay; as clock advances the recency term grows and BLA falls). No new math.
- SWEEP: iterate live (t_invalid==0, not already demoted) T3 records; compute current BLA; DEMOTE a record only when BLA < BLA_FLOOR AND importance <= IMP_FLOOR (low-activation AND low-importance -- a high-importance record never demotes on age alone; the GA decay x BLA composition). Thresholds are fixed-point integer consts (like SCALE/TOKEN_QUOTA) for deterministic replay.
- Two levels, both preserving the T2 lossless floor:
  - DEMOTE (new demoted flag / tier): excluded from recall STAGE 1 candidate-gen (add `if r.demoted { continue }`), shrinking the active lexical index, BUT still addressable via M_MEM_READ over T2 (MEMORY-SPEC s6.1: moves down a tier T3->archival, does not disappear).
  - TOMBSTONE (existing t_invalid flip): for the most-decayed; bitemporal history preserved (s6.2 tombstone-not-delete).
- INVARIANT: the forget sweep NEVER pops/truncates t2.log -- it only flips flags / t_invalid. Hard delete stays a privileged human-gated op (s6.3), explicitly OUT of M17.
recon.abi:
PURELY ADDITIVE BODIES -- no new method numbers, no ABI break, the M11 closed method set stays frozen (M_BLOCK_READ=31 is the highest; the M16 test already asserts method 33 == BadMethod and an intact closed set).
- M17 EXTENDS the existing MemSubstrate::consolidate(op,a,b,c) op-space reached through the SAME M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20 method (Rights::CONSOLIDATE=1<<9, required_right + dispatch branch at caps.rs ~L412/~L900 already plumb all four inline args): today op 0=tombstone, 1=link-stub, 2=T1-GC. Add op=3 = run one bounded consolidation_cycle (distill+reflect+forget), op=4 = reflect-only, op=5 = forget-sweep, and FILL op=1 with the real supersedes/cites link write. args[0]=op selector, args[1..3]=ids/params (the existing convention).
- caps.rs needs NO change (method, right, gate, dispatch branch all exist).
- New SAFE fields on existing structs (zero unsafe, #![forbid(unsafe_code)]): MemRecord gains links:Vec<(u8,u64)> (typed supersedes/cites/relates) and demoted:bool (or tier:u8); MemSubstrate/Quota gains imp_accum:u32 + last_consolidated_epoch:u64; add() accumulates importance; recall STAGE 1 gains the demoted skip.
- lib.rs adds kernel-side FACADES only (no dispatch methods): agent_consolidate_cycle(task) (the agent_mem_dispatch clone driving M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op=3 through the chokepoint) and optional read-only agent_mem_accumulator(task) so the self-test can observe the ~150 crossing. The daemon TASK rides the existing scheduler_spawn; the model-bridged reflect rides the existing agent_model_open/agent_model_dispatch. No new infer.rs API.
recon.selftest_plan:
A self-test block in kernel/src/main.rs inserted between the M16 marker (L1998 "M16: infer OK") and tb_hal::halt() (L2000), ending with tb_hal::serial_write_str("M17: consolidate OK\n"); -- mirroring the M13 WITNESS-A/B structure with an m17_fail(why) helper and the disp() dispatch closure. Steps:
1. SEED: WRITE several T2 episodes via M_MEM_WRITE on a home holding READ|WRITE|RECALL|CONSOLIDATE -- including near-duplicates on the SAME token (to be distilled/merged) and high-importance writes whose importance sums PAST the ~150 accumulator threshold (to fire the trigger). Capture ids.
2. FORCE CYCLE: dispatch M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op=3 (bounded cycle) -- assert Ok and a non-zero affected count; optionally assert agent_mem_accumulator crossed ~150.
3. ASSERT DISTILL: the near-duplicates merged into a durable T3 record linked by supersedes/cites -- M_MEM_RECALL on the token returns a single winning distilled record; the superseded one is demoted/linked (M_OBJECT_INSPECT==MemoryRecord, M_MEM_READ==expected id).
4. ASSERT REFLECT: a NEW insight T3 record was produced with cites links to its sources; if the model-bridged path runs, assert insight_token == digest ^ 0xA110_C0DE (deterministic mock). Recall a reflection query token -> the insight id.
5. ASSERT FORGET: a low-activation low-importance record was DEMOTED (absent from a subsequent M_MEM_RECALL candidate set) and/or tombstoned -- BUT still ADDRESSABLE: M_MEM_READ of its id over T2 STILL returns its value (the key T2-floor-intact assertion).
6. T2 FLOOR INTEGRITY: read back an original episode id post-cycle -> still returns its written value (nothing destroyed).
7. CONSOLIDATE-RIGHT-GATED PATH: on a home minted WITHOUT CONSOLIDATE (READ|RECALL, the M13 home_ro precedent) M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op=3 -> Denied; AND the daemon-driven path via agent_consolidate_cycle(agent_c) -> Denied (agent_c's born-with mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL lacks CONSOLIDATE, mirroring the existing M13 "agent C consolidate Denied" line).
8. OPTIONAL CONCURRENCY WITNESS: scheduler_spawn the daemon (STACK_DAEMON.take()), timer_rearm() briefly, confirm a foreground M_MEM_RECALL still returns within the run window while involuntary_switch_count() advances, then timer_disarm() before the verdict (the M9 armed-window pattern). Print intermediate "mem: distilled + reflected + demoted, T2 floor intact" lines like M13. All M2-M16 markers re-print (cumulative DoD).
recon.integration_points:
- kernel/src/main.rs: new M17 self-test block at L1998->L2000 (after "M16: infer OK", before halt()), terminating with the marker "M17: consolidate OK\n". Reuses the already-spawned agent_c/agent_d and the STACK_*.take() arena pattern (add a STACK_DAEMON static + a daemon-entry fn if the scheduled-task witness is included); m17_fail helper mirrors m13_fail/m16_fail.
- crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs: extend consolidate() op-space (ops 3/4/5 + fill op 1); add private consolidation_cycle()/reflect()/forget_sweep()/distill(); add safe fields (links, demoted/tier, imp_accum, last_consolidated_epoch); accumulate importance in add(); add the demoted skip in recall STAGE 1. Stays #![forbid(unsafe_code)].
- crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs: NO change -- M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20 (L356), Rights::CONSOLIDATE=1<<9 (L118), required_right mapping (L412) and the M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE dispatch branch (L900) already route args[0..3] into consolidate().
- crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs: add agent_consolidate_cycle(task) + optional agent_mem_accumulator(task) facades (agent_mem_dispatch ~L1399 clones); daemon task uses existing scheduler_spawn (~L829)/irq_unmask (~L904)/timer_rearm (~L886)/timer_disarm (~L893); model-bridged reflect uses existing agent_model_open (~L1747)/agent_model_dispatch (~L1772). AgentProcess already carries memory_home (~L1205).
- crates/tb-hal/src/infer.rs: NO change -- reflect rides the existing deterministic model:mock/echo backend.
- Spec basis: docs/ROADMAP-V2.md s3 M17 (L269, marker "M17: consolidate OK", deps M13/M16/M9, "Zero new unsafe"); docs/MEMORY-SPEC.md s4 (consolidation/reflection, importance-accumulator threshold 150, async kswapd daemon) + s6 (forgetting: demote-via-decay, tombstone-not-delete); docs/SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s3 (default-on reflection) + s4 (sleep-time BULK, every-N/on-idle/on-memory-pressure triggers).
risks: Scheduled-daemon concurrency vs the disarmed-timer discipline: M13-M16 self-tests all run with the timer DISARMED (interrupts masked). A real preemptive daemon (armed timer) re-introduces the RefCell-reentrancy hazard the AGENTS registry forbids -- the daemon's borrow_mut on a substrate must not overlap a foreground dispatch on the SAME home. Mitigate: bound the cycle single-&mut-at-a-time, drive the deterministic marker synchronously, keep the armed-window concurrency witness to a separate agent/home.

T2 lossless-floor destruction: the forget sweep must NEVER pop/truncate t2.log -- only flip t_invalid/demoted flags. A demote that drops the canonical record breaks the 'still addressable' guarantee and the lossless invariant.

Determinism: the ~150 accumulator trigger and the BLA/importance demote thresholds must be fixed-point integer consts (like SCALE/TOKEN_QUOTA). Any clock/age off-by-one changes which records demote and flakes the CI assertion.

Reflect model-seam scope creep: wiring the model INTO mem.rs would couple the substrate to infer.rs and force the daemon's INVOKE_MODEL cap through the wrong layer. Keep the model pass at the daemon-task level; mem.rs reflect stays a pure deterministic digest.

ABI surface creep: adding a NEW method number instead of reusing M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE op-selectors would change the M11 closed method set, perturbing the M16 'method 33 == BadMethod'/closed-set assertions and the frozen-boundary proof. Reuse op=3/4/5 under M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20.

Distill semantics mismatch: today T3 is 1:1 with T2 (add() writes a record per episode), so 'distill T2->T3' is really dedup/merge-within-T3 + link. Re-deriving T3 from scratch risks dropping instant-RYW or desyncing the lexical inverted index (postings must stay consistent with demoted/merged records).

Write-amplification quota interaction: reflect/distill WRITE new insight records consuming TOKEN_QUOTA; an unbounded reflection could exhaust quota and fail-closed mid-cycle. The cycle must respect the existing Quota and bound records-written per cycle.
